11.reason: [This loop may access out-of-bounds st[100] when stack is full (all indices 0-99 non-zero), causing undefined behavior. Should stop at i<100.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This loop uses i to find an empty slot, but i is declared outside and reused, causing potential issues with the push logic. Also, using st[i]!=0 as condition fails if 0 is a valid pushed value.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: This loop assumes that a value of 0 indicates an empty slot, but 0 could be a valid pushed value, causing incorrect behavior. Also, the loop does not check for array bounds, potentially accessing st[100] which is out of bounds.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This loop may access out-of-bounds st[100] when stack is full (all indices 0-99 non-zero), causing undefined behavior. Should stop at i<100.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  // Error: out-of-bounds access when i reaches 100 (stack full) because condition does not check i<100; should be for(i=0; i<100 && st[i]!=0; i++);
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Out-of-bounds array access when stack is full (i reaches 100), should check i<100 first.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Accesses st[100] out of bounds when stack is full; loop condition should check i < 100 first.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This loop assumes that the stack is stored contiguously from index 0 and uses 0 to indicate unused slots. However, if a pushed value is 0, it will be treated as an empty slot, causing incorrect behavior. The problem does not restrict pushed values from being 0, so using 0 as a sentinel is invalid.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [This loop assumes that the stack is stored contiguously from index 0, but after popping (setting an element to 0), there may be zeros in the middle, causing the push to overwrite or skip positions incorrectly. The stack should be managed with a top pointer, not by searching for the first zero.]

15.reason: The format string has a trailing space, which may cause input reading issues. Also, it does not check if the stack is full before reading the value.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The format string has a trailing space, which may cause input reading issues. Also, it does not check if the stack is full before reading the value.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: The format string "%lld " has a trailing space, which will cause scanf to consume any following whitespace, potentially skipping the next input token. Also, the variable 'a' is declared as int but the stack stores long long int, and the input for push values is read as %lld, but the operation code is read as %d, causing type mismatch issues.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  // Error: when stack is full (i==100), the integer value after '1' is not read from input, causing the next scanf to consume it as an operation number. Should still read the integer to discard it.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Trailing space in scanf format string causes it to wait for non-whitespace input, potentially hanging or misreading.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Trailing space in format string causes scanf to wait indefinitely for non-whitespace input; should be "%lld".
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The space after %lld in scanf can cause input parsing issues by consuming extra whitespace unnecessarily and potentially hanging or misreading input. Also, this approach of using 0-initialized array to track stack size fails if 0 is a valid push value.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Using "%lld " with a space at the end can cause input parsing issues, especially if the next input is immediately following without whitespace. Also, this approach of using 0 to denote empty slots fails when 0 is a valid value to push (though problem says pushed integers are not 0, but logic is still flawed due to non-contiguous tracking).]

18.reason: [The condition order is wrong: it checks st[j]==0 before j>=0, so when j becomes -1, st[-1] is accessed out-of-bounds. Should be j>=0 && st[j]==0.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This loop searches from the top (index 99) for a non-zero element, but the stack may have gaps due to the push logic, leading to incorrect pop behavior.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: This loop assumes that a value of 0 indicates an empty slot, but 0 could be a valid pushed value, causing incorrect pop behavior. Also, the loop starts from index 99, which is incorrect for a stack that should pop from the top (last pushed element).]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The condition order is wrong: it checks st[j]==0 before j>=0, so when j becomes -1, st[-1] is accessed out-of-bounds. Should be j>=0 && st[j]==0.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  // Error: accesses st[-1] when j becomes -1 (empty stack) because st[j]==0 is evaluated before checking j>=0; should be for(j=99; j>=0 && st[j]==0; j--);
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Incorrect short-circuit evaluation order; st[j] is accessed when j=-1, causing out-of-bounds access. j>=0 should be checked first.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Evaluates st[j] before checking j>=0, leading to out-of-bounds access st[-1] when stack is empty; conditions should be swapped.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Same issue: assumes 0 means empty. If 0 was pushed, this will skip it incorrectly, leading to popping the wrong element or reporting "error" when the stack isn't actually empty.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [This searches from the end for a non-zero element, which assumes that all valid elements are at the high end, but pushes fill from the bottom. This leads to incorrect pop behavior — it doesn't simulate a LIFO stack correctly. For example, after pushing 5, 10, the stack should pop 10 then 5, but this will look from index 99 downward and miss them if they're at indices 0 and 1.]

13.reason: Fails to consume the integer to be pushed from the input buffer, causing subsequent reads to be misaligned.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Fails to consume the integer to be pushed from the input buffer, causing subsequent reads to be misaligned.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Fails to consume the integer value for the push operation, causing input stream desynchronization.

23.reason: [Setting popped element to 0 breaks future operations if 0 is a valid value. This design conflates data values with metadata (empty vs filled).]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Setting popped element to 0 breaks future operations if 0 is a valid value. This design conflates data values with metadata (empty vs filled).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Setting st[j]=0 breaks the contiguous assumption and introduces gaps. Combined with the flawed push logic, this causes future operations to misbehave. The stack should use a top index instead of relying on zero/non-zero values.]
